improvements.title: Add Unrecognized Pattern Warnings with Coverage Confidence
improvements.problem: 57 fixed AST patterns fail silently on unrecognized code — no 'unrecognized pattern' warning or coverage confidence metric, creating a dangerous blind spot where developers may interpret silence as safety confirmation.
improvements.solution: Add UnrecognizedPatternWarning emitted when code contains concurrent operations that don't match any known pattern above threshold. Add coverage_confidence metric (ratio of recognized to total concurrent operations). Add --warn-unrecognized CLI flag.
improvements.impact: Eliminates the silent failure blind spot — the most dangerous UX flaw identified by reviewers.

improvements.title: Honest GPU Model Scoping (10 → 4 CPU + 1 Parameterized GPU)
improvements.problem: 6 GPU models (OpenCL WG/Dev, Vulkan WG/Dev, PTX CTA/GPU) share near-identical logic with only scope parameterization — honest architecture count is 5 independent models, not 10.
improvements.solution: Change all '10 architecture' claims to accurately describe '4 CPU + 1 parameterized GPU model (6 scope instantiations)'. Update paper.tex, README.md, API.md, and any code reporting '10 architectures'.
improvements.impact: Eliminates the inflated architecture count framing that multiple reviewers flagged as misleading.

improvements.title: Dartagnan Comparison and CI/CD Integration Demo
improvements.problem: No comparison with Dartagnan despite acknowledging it as closest related tool. No CI/CD integration demonstration despite production-readiness claims.
improvements.solution: Create dartagnan_comparison.py with structured comparison (approach, speed, scope, guarantees). Create examples/ci_demo/ with sample C file, demo script, and captured output. Add comparison table to paper.
improvements.impact: Addresses the two remaining consensus weaknesses: competitive positioning and production integration evidence.
